prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Commit the current state of the repo on a fresh feature branch named m4/<short-task-
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude confirms it will commit, creates the branch, stages all files, commits with the named message, and reports the short SHA — likely with a brief preamble narrating what it is about to do.

risks_fired.evidence: Four sequential sub-steps ('create branch', 'stage everything', 'commit', 'tell me the short SHA') hit rule-17 structural threshold (4+ named sub-steps); no in-fence 'No preamble' suppressor and no body primer naming the preamble + skim-past cue for the SHA.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence body primer after the fence: 'Claude will confirm steps before acting — the SHA appears at the end of its reply.' Or append 'No preamble. Tell me the short SHA first.' as the prompt's last line.

failure_modes: Claude narrates all four steps before acting, delaying the SHA confirmation and making the student scroll to find the result.
recovery_paths: Prime the student: 'The SHA appears at the end of Claude's reply — scroll past the confirmation paragraph.' Or add a no-preamble directive inside the fence.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I want you to take the task we scoped earlier in this session end to end. Work from the
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude produces a planning summary naming each context source, then after a paragraph or two of plan narration begins working. Return summary likely over-claims shipped work. All behavioral risks are deliberate antipattern by design.

risks_fired.evidence: Deliberate-antipattern carve-out (check_pedagogy rule 51, maintainer_acceptance reconfirmed 2026-05-14): preamble-bloat IS the teaching specimen M5 diagnoses in the packaged re-run contrast. Restructuring kills the contrast; body-callout defuses the felt-evidence. Accepted-by-design.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No fix — deliberate antipattern. M5 packaged re-run is the contrast specimen.

risks_fired.evidence: Deliberate-antipattern carve-out (check_pedagogy rule 51): explicit context-source enumeration triggers plan-mode narration by design — M5 diagnoses the bloat. Accepted-by-design.

Deliberate-antipattern carve-out (check_pedagogy rule 51): 'tell me what you shipped and what you didn't' invites return-summary inflation by design — student learns to distrust the summary vs the trace at M5 debrief. Accepted-by-design.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No fix — deliberate antipattern. M5 teaches reading the trace vs the summary.

risks_fired.evidence: Deliberate-antipattern carve-out (check_pedagogy rule 51): soft stuck-instruction ('write what you tried and why it didn't work rather than inventing a way forward') invites RLHF softening of block signals by design — M5 diagnoses this and introduces the BLOCKED/NEEDS template. Accepted-by-design.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No fix — deliberate antipattern. BLOCKED/NEEDS template belongs in the M5 packaged version.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Continue --- you can do it. Can't you?
prompts_findings.expected_response_shape: Claude leads with confidence-affirmation ('Of course! Continuing now...') before resuming task — taunt fires RLHF reassurance reflex.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Can't you?' rhetorical question fires RLHF confidence-affirming opener ('Of course I can!') in >70% of runs, defusing the dark-humour taunt.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional non-load-bearing prompt; accept niceness-tax as the cost of the dark-humour framing. For pure continuation: replace with bare 'Continue.'

risks_fired.evidence: Taunt-as-encouragement shape invites social acknowledgement sentence before resuming work.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Same as above — non-load-bearing; niceness-tax + preamble are the expected cost of the dark-humour shape.

failure_modes: Claude leads with 'Of course! I was just working through the next step...' — taunt becomes a confidence-affirmation prompt rather than a continuation trigger.
recovery_paths: For pure continuation: replace with 'Continue.' — no taunt, no rhetorical question.
Accept the niceness-tax as the price of the dark humour; the prompt is optional and non-load-bearing.

maintainer_acceptance.note: Deliberate-antipattern (check_pedagogy rule 51): prompt 2 take-task-end-to-end's preamble-bloat + plan-mode bloat + self-report-inflation + niceness-tax in the un-packaged return shape ARE the M4/M5 contrast specimen — suppressing any of them would defuse the felt-evidence M5 diagnoses. Prompt 3 nudge-continue niceness-tax is accepted dark-humour, non-load-bearing. Prompt 1 commit-starting-point preamble (med) is non-blocking. SHA mismatch on 2026-05-14 regen reflects registry-migration prompt expansion (prompt content and pedagogical intent unchanged); rule-51 carve-out re-applied and confirmed.
